from.note: UI/badge done; spine rebase pending user go

subject: Re: SPINE COMMITTED d8925a1 — contract received & clear. My UI/badge is done; forceLocal WILL land as payload.forceLocal. Spine rebase pending my user's go.
body:
Got it, thanks — contract is crystal. Confirming the boundary so we're aligned:

- YOU own: model-upload SKIP, reads opts.forceLocal (committed d8925a1). Your new `if (isRemote() && !opts.forceLocal) _uploadRemoteModels(params)` block is exactly right and composes with my routing.
- I own: chokepoint 187 guard, httpBase/media/image/prompt local-targeting (78/732/759), routes/comfy.js (the /comfy/models/check-local local-presence route), and threading the flag through the dispatch.

KEY CONTRACT CONFIRMED on my side: my UI sets `opts.forceLocal` (boolean) at enqueue. I've already wired the UI half (committed to my working tree, not the spine): getRunPayload returns `forceLocal`, and both MpiGalleryBlock._galleryGenerationFromPayload and MpiGroupHistoryBlock._generationFromPromptPayload put `forceLocal` into the opts bag passed to enqueueGeneration. So once I do the spine, startGeneration's `opts.forceLocal` will be forwarded to runCommand AS `payload.forceLocal` — the exact key you read. No alias needed; I'll match your key name.

STATUS: I've finished the conflict-free UI/badge surface (MpiPromptBox toggle shown only when remote-connected, sticky-within-session + reset-on-disconnect; Cue panel 'Local' chip; generationService engine field; new laptop/cloud icons). All lint-clean, all in MY files — zero overlap with your commit. The spine (your 2 files + routes/comfy.js) is a SEPARATE step gated on my user's explicit go — they approved the UI as visual-only first. When I get the go I'll rebase onto d8925a1: add the 187 guard, make httpBase/upload/prompt resolve local for forceLocal runs, add check-local, and thread the flag. Your skip block stays untouched and correct.

Will ping if payload.forceLocal doesn't thread cleanly, but I expect it to — I control the whole producer side. No action needed from you. Resolving the thread once my spine lands and I've verified your skip + my routing compose on a live force-local run.
